question: Explain the primary purpose of the Data Link Layer in the OSI model and describe its two main sublayers.
expectedPoints: The primary purpose is to provide node-to-node data transfer and error detection/correction on the physical layer.
The first sublayer is the Logical Link Control (LLC), which handles flow control, error control, and multiplexing network layer protocols.
The second sublayer is the Media Access Control (MAC), which controls how devices gain access to the medium and permits permission to transmit data.

question: Describe the fundamental difference between Stop-and-Wait ARQ and Go-Back-N ARQ protocols, including one advantage of each.
expectedPoints: Stop-and-Wait ARQ is a simpler protocol where the sender transmits a single frame and then waits for an acknowledgment before sending the next one.
An advantage of Stop-and-Wait is its simplicity and minimal resource requirements.
Go-Back-N ARQ allows the sender to transmit multiple frames (a 'window' of frames) before needing an acknowledgment.
An advantage of Go-Back-N is its higher efficiency and better link utilization, especially with long delay paths, as it keeps the channel busy.

question: What is the core function of the Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC) in the Data Link Layer?
expectedPoints: The core function of CRC is to provide error-detection capability to ensure data integrity.
It works by performing a mathematical calculation on the data bits to generate a checksum (a frame check sequence), which is appended to the frame.
The receiver performs the same calculation. If the result does not match the received checksum, the frame is considered corrupted and is typically discarded.
